[{"title": "Korean aegyo speech shows systematic F1 increase to signal childlike qualities", "authors": ["Ji-eun Kim", "Volker Dellwo"], "summary": "Korean aegyo is a socially recognized childlike speaking style used predominantly in romantic interactions among adults. This study examined vowel space modification in aegyo by analyzing formant frequencies from twelve Seoul Korean speakers who produced identical scripts in aegyo and non-aegyo styles. Results show that aegyo speech features a significant increase in F1 values across vowels and selective fronting of front vowels, leading to vowel space expansion but mainly a shift to higher F1. These findings suggest that adult speakers stylize childlike speech by imitating the shorter vocal tract of children, mainly through global vowel lowering and partial fronting.", "excerpt": "Korean aegyo is a socially recognized childlike speaking style used predominantly in romantic interactions among adults. They are shown to be special cases of elements of the group G, that contains both displacements and squeezing transformations. Acting with them on the vacuum we get various classes of coherent states, which we call bifractional coherent states. They are special classes of squeezed states which can be used for interpolation between various quantities in phase space methods. Using them we introduce bifractional Wigner functions A(?, ?; ??, ??), which are a two-dimensional continuum of functions, and reduce to Wigner and Weyl functions in special cases. We also introduce bifractional Q-functions, and bifractional P-functions. messages.content:
"애교"는 주로 성인간의 낭만적 상호작용에서 사용되는 한국의 사회적으로 인정받는 아기 같은 말투입니다. 연구에 따르면, 애교 말투는 모음 빈도를 조절하여 아기 같은 특성을 신호하는 경향이 있습니다. 서울 한국어 화자들이 애교 스타일과 일반 스타일로 동일한 원고를 낭독하는 실험에서, 애교에는 모음의 F1 값이 유의미하게 증가하고, 앞 모음의 선택적 전방화가 관찰되었습니다. 이러한 결과는 성인 화자들이 아기의 짧은 목소리 길이를 모방하여 아기 같은 말을 스타일화한다고 제안합니다.

더 많은 정보를 원하신다면, 자세한 내용은 이 논문에서 확인하실 수 있습니다: [Korean aegyo speech shows systematic F1 increase to signal childlike qualities](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25133v1).
can you translate what you said into English?

"Aegyo" is a socially recognized childlike speaking style in Korea, primarily used in romantic interactions among adults. Research indicates that aegyo speech tends to signal childlike qualities by modifying vowel frequencies. In a study involving Seoul Korean speakers who read the same script in both aegyo and non-aegyo styles, significant increases in F1 values of vowels and selective fronting of front vowels were observed in aegyo. These findings suggest that adult speakers stylize childlike speech by mimicking the shorter vocal tract of children.

For more information, you can check the paper: [Korean aegyo speech shows systematic F1 increase to signal childlike qualities](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25133v1).
